revision_reviewed: rev-5 plan, post rev-4 fix set (uncommitted at review time, committed in same revision as this JSON)
summary: First review pass with no BLOCKERs across all four reviewers. The 3-way convergence pattern that drove rev-3 and rev-4 was broken: rev-4's property-test rewrite (type-agnostic path-aware `!=` invariant) is confirmed by Quality and Systems as a genuine structural inversion — the comparison site now uses Python `__ne__` which is defined for every object, so there is no equivalent surface to silently exempt an annotation class via a type filter. All four lanes (Reality, Architecture, Quality, Systems) returned APPROVED_WITH_WARNINGS. Six actionable warnings folded in-revision; four warnings deferred with rationale (see Appendix E of the plan).

B1_property_test_rewrite.convergence: Quality and Systems independently characterised the rev-4 design as a 'genuine structural inversion' that eliminates the entire silent-pass family, not just the isinstance(str) mode. The Python `__ne__` comparison site has no equivalent of the type filter that prior versions admitted.
exotic_modes_examined_and_dismissed: Container substituted with string sentinel: value_provider(redacted) raises TypeError; no defensive except absorbs it; test fails loudly
Both raw and redacted None: explicit single allowed skip rule; over-aggressive redaction is out of scope
Hypothesis generates value equal to summarizer output: false-positive on redactor correctness, not silent-pass; acceptable noise
sensitive_nodes built once outside @given: deterministic walker; correct

convergence_findings.finding: Property test rev-4 design is a structural inversion of the prior failure-mode family
convergence_findings.treatment: Both lanes independently arrived at 'genuine structural inversion' language. The rev-3/rev-4 pattern of converging at the same line range is BROKEN at this pass.
convergence_findings.pattern_note: First review pass since this plan's history began that has no convergent silent-pass mode in the property test region. Each fix between rev-3 and rev-5 reduced the comparison-site surface: rev-3 removed ad-hoc path parsing; rev-4 removed isinstance/None-skip/except filters; rev-5 hardened against latent modes (empty container, nested Sensitive). The final invariant uses Python __ne__ which has no operator-level filter.

recommendations_for_implementer: Read Appendices C, D, E IN ORDER before editing Task 19's property test. The institutional memory chain is: rev-2 string-substring shape → rev-3 path-traversal fix → rev-4 isinstance-filter family → rev-5 type-agnostic !=. Editing the test without reading this chain risks re-introducing a closed silent-pass mode.

The six-item implementer cross-check appended to Task 19's task body is load-bearing. A code-quality reviewer signing off without re-checking each item is a process gap (cited in the task body itself).

The collection-time verification step (`pytest --collect-only -q`) MUST run before the property-test commit. Hypothesis `ResolutionFailed` at collection time disables a parametrize entry without an obvious signal.

next_steps.rev6_review_scope: If a future rev-6 review is requested before Phase 2 implementation begins, it should be a focused re-check of (a) Task 10 sixth adequacy assertion correctness, (b) Task 1 short-circuit test, and (c) Task 19 event() instrumentation — the three structural additions in rev-5. No reviewer flagged a region needing broad rework, so a full 4-reviewer pass is not required.
